mcqs.question: Which method is used to create a new HttpSession or retrieve an existing one?
mcqs.explanation: request.getSession() returns the current session or creates a new one if none exists. request.getSession(false) returns null if there is no current session.

mcqs.question: Which method is used to store data in an HttpSession?
mcqs.explanation: setAttribute(String name, Object value) binds an object to the session with the given name. getAttribute(name) retrieves it.

mcqs.question: What is the default session tracking mechanism used by servlet containers?
mcqs.explanation: Servlet containers use cookies (specifically the JSESSIONID cookie) as the default mechanism. If cookies are disabled, URL rewriting can be used as a fallback.

mcqs.question: What does session.invalidate() do?

options.text: Removes a single attribute from the session
Destroys the entire session and all its data

mcqs.explanation: invalidate() destroys the entire session, removing all attributes. After this call, any attempt to use the session throws IllegalStateException.

mcqs.question: Which method is used for URL rewriting when cookies might be disabled?
mcqs.explanation: response.encodeURL(url) appends the session ID to the URL if the client does not support cookies. If cookies are enabled, the URL is returned unchanged.

mcqs.question: What happens when request.getSession(false) is called and no session exists?

options.text: An empty session object is returned

mcqs.explanation: getSession(false) returns null if no session currently exists. getSession() or getSession(true) would create a new session if one does not exist.

mcqs.question: How is session timeout configured in web.xml?
mcqs.explanation: In web.xml, session timeout is configured within <session-config> using <session-timeout> with the value in minutes. In code, setMaxInactiveInterval() takes seconds.
